tooltips.fovOfSensor: Field of View of the Sensor

errorMsgs.noSecondary: No secondary satellite selected. Right-click a satellite or use [ ] keys.
errorMsgs.targetNotFound: Target satellite not found in catalog.
errorMsgs.targetSameAsSource: Target satellite cannot be the same as source.
errorMsgs.fovRange: Field of View must be a number between 0 and 180 degrees.
errorMsgs.redRange: Red color value must be a number between 0 and 1.
errorMsgs.greenRange: Green color value must be a number between 0 and 1.
errorMsgs.blueRange: Blue color value must be a number between 0 and 1.
errorMsgs.opacityRange: Opacity value must be a number between 0 and 1.

help.overview: Satellite FOV draws sensor cones from a satellite so you can see what it can see: either a nadir cone toward Earth (Earth Center) or a crosslink cone pointed at another satellite (Sat-to-Sat). Set the cone angle and color, and the volume renders live in the 3D view.
help.imgAlt: The Satellite Field of View menu with its Earth Center and Sat-to-Sat tabs
help.imgCaption: Configure the cone angle and color, then toggle cones per satellite.
help.tabs: The Earth Center tab manages nadir pointing cones: set the default Field of View (Degrees) and color, press C to toggle a cone on the selected satellite, and manage every active cone in the Active Sensors list. The Sat-to-Sat tab builds a cone from a source satellite toward a target: enter a Target Catalog Number or click Use Secondary Sat, then Create Sat-to-Sat Cone; V toggles a cone toward the current secondary satellite.
help.howToUse:
1. Select a satellite; the bottom icon is disabled until one is selected.
  2. Open the menu and adjust the cone angle, color, and opacity. Values apply to new cones.
  3. Press C (or use the menu) to toggle an Earth pointing cone on the selected satellite.
  4. For a crosslink, open the Sat-to-Sat tab, pick a target, and click Create Sat-to-Sat Cone.
  5. Use the reset buttons to clear all cones of either type.
help.tip1: Cones persist as you select other satellites, so you can build up a constellation wide coverage picture one satellite at a time.
help.tip2: Set the secondary satellite by right-clicking a satellite or with the [ and ] keys; Use Secondary Sat then fills the target automatically.
help.shortcutToggleCone: Toggle an Earth pointing FOV cone on the selected satellite
help.shortcutToggleS2s: Toggle a cone toward the secondary satellite
